text.title: success-metrics-definition Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for success-metrics-definition. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every primary KPI must have a paired leading indicator. For example, organic traffic (output) pairs with published posts or backlinks built (inputs). Growth rate (output) pairs with trial signups or feature adoption (inputs).
rule.rationale: Leading indicators allow you to course-correct before lagging metrics move. They give you signal weeks or months earlier. Without inputs, you are flying blind and can't optimize the engine.

rule.statement: Every metric must pass the actionability test. If you cannot articulate what action you would take if the metric dropped 50%, it is vanity and should be retired.
rule.rationale: Vanity metrics create false confidence. They feel like progress when they aren't. Actionable metrics drive decisions and resource allocation. Teams that track vanity metrics optimize for the wrong things and waste months.
